critic_raw: The equipment failure prediction experiment is well-designed. Two residual concerns: maintenance-log-based labeling treats near-failures as negatives, which is an inherent limitation. Rare machine types may lack sufficient test-set failures for reliable stratified evaluation.
all_issues_raised: Near-failure periods with anomalous readings but no logged failure are labeled negative, a limitation of maintenance-log-based labeling
Rare machine types may have insufficient failures in the 6-month test set for reliable per-machine-type F1 scores

round_transcripts.critic: The equipment failure prediction experiment is well-designed. Two residual concerns: maintenance-log-based labeling treats near-failures as negatives, which is an inherent limitation. Rare machine types may lack sufficient test-set failures for reliable stratified evaluation.
round_transcripts.defender: Both concerns are acknowledged by the design itself. The maintenance log labeling scheme is standard for predictive maintenance and the design scopes conclusions appropriately. Rare machine types with insufficient failures in the test set are flagged as limitations with per-machine-type F1 treated as a diagnostic rather than a definitive metric. The design's transparency about these limitations is appropriate.
